title: Worked Example - Flex Container & Flex Items

content.problemStatement:
This example will visually show you how Flexbox arranges elements inside a container. You’ll see how a navbar uses flex to place items side by side and how a hero section can center content both horizontally and vertically.

**How It Works**
- **Navbar (Flex Row):**  
  The `.navbar` is set to `display: flex;`.  
  - `justify-content: space-between;` pushes the logo to the left and the links to the right.  
  - `align-items: center;` makes sure everything lines up neatly in the middle, even if the height changes.
- **Nav Links (Side by Side):**  
  Inside `.nav-links`, we use `display: flex;` with a gap.  
  This makes each link sit next to each other in a row instead of stacking.
- **Hero Section (Centering Content):**  
  The `.hero` is another flex container.  
  - `justify-content: center;` puts the content in the middle horizontally.  
  - `align-items: center;` puts the content in the middle vertically.  
---
**Try Yourself**
- **Change Alignment in Navbar:**  
  In `styles.css`, change `justify-content: space-between;` to `center;`.  
  → The logo and links will move together into the center.
- **Adjust Hero Position:**  
  Change `align-items: center;` to `flex-start;`.  
  → The hero text will shift to the top of the section instead of the middle.
- **Experiment with Spacing:**  
  Increase or decrease the gap in `.nav-links` to see how spacing between links changes.
constraints:
- $1 \leq T \leq 100$
- $1 \leq N \leq 100$
- $1 \leq M \leq N\cdot(N-1)/2$
- $1 \leq u_i, v_i \leq N$
- $u_i \neq v_i$ for each $1 \leq i \leq M$.
- The sum of $N$ over all test cases won't exceed $100$.
content.inputFormat:
- The first line of input will contain a single integer $T$, denoting the number of test cases.
- Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.
    - The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers $N$ and $M$ — the number of vertices and edges of the graph, respectively.
    - The next $M$ lines describe the edges. The $i$-th of these $M$ lines contains two space-separated integers $u_i$ and $v_i$, denoting an edge between $u_i$ and $v_i$.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output on a new line the number of good subgraphs of $G$, modulo $10^9 + 7$.
